subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Swelling and the RICE Method
description: One-minute skill: Swelling and the RICE Method.

hook.text: You sprain your ankle and it balloons to twice its size. The swelling has a purpose.

buildup.text: Fluid rushes to the injury site carrying immune cells and nutrients for repair.

discovery.text: RICE—Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation—limits excess swelling that could slow healing.

twist.text: Too much swelling compresses nerves and blood vessels, creating more damage than it prevents.

climax.text: Some experts now prefer PEACE & LOVE—a newer acronym emphasizing active recovery.

punchline.text: Some swelling helps. Too much hurts. Balance is everything.

quiz.question: What does RICE stand for in first aid?
